Go to the documentation of this file.

00020  * ARE DISCLAIMED.  IN NO EVENT SHALL THE UNIVERSITY OR CONTRIBUTORS BE LIABLE

00022  * DAMAGES (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS
00023  * OR SERVICES; LOSS OF USE, DATA, OR PROFITS; OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION)
00024  * HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, STRICT
00025  * LIABILITY, OR TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE OR OTHERWISE) ARISING IN ANY WAY
00026  * OUT OF THE USE OF THIS SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF
00027  * SUCH DAMAGE.
00028  */
00029 
00030 /*
00031  * psort - parallel sort.
00032  *
00033  * This is loosely based on some real parallel sort benchmarks, but
00034  * because of various limitations of OS/161 it is massively
00035  * inefficient. But that's ok; the goal is to stress the VM and buffer
00036  * cache.
00037  */
00038 
00039 #include <sys/types.h>
00040 #include <sys/stat.h>
00041 #include <sys/wait.h>
00042 #include <stdio.h>
00043 #include <stdarg.h>
00044 #include <stdlib.h>
00045 #include <string.h>
00046 #include <assert.h>
00047 #include <unistd.h>
00048 #include <fcntl.h>
00049 #include <errno.h>
00050 
00051 #ifndef RANDOM_MAX
00052 /* Note: this is correct for OS/161 but not for some Unix C libraries */
00053 #define RANDOM_MAX RAND_MAX
00054 #endif
00055 
00056 #define PATH_KEYS    "sortkeys"
00057 #define PATH_SORTED  "output"
00058 #define PATH_TESTDIR "psortdir"
00059 #define PATH_RANDOM  "rand:"
00060 
00061 #define WORKNUM      (128*1024)
00062 
00063 
00064 static int workspace[WORKNUM];
00065 
00066 static const char *progname;
00067 
00068 static int numprocs = 4;
00069 static int numkeys = 10000;
00070 static long randomseed = 15432753;
00071 
00072 static off_t correctsize;
00073 static unsigned long checksum;
00074 
00075 #define NOBODY (-1)
00076 static int me = NOBODY;
00077 
00079 
00080 static
00081 void
00082 sortints(int *v, int num)
00083 {
00084         int pivotval, pivotpoint, pivotcount;
00085         int frontpos, readpos, endpos, i, j;
00086         int tmp;
00087 
00088         if (num < 2) {
00089                 return;
00090         }
00091 
00092         pivotpoint = num/2;
00093         pivotval = v[pivotpoint];
00094         pivotcount = 0;
00095 
00096         frontpos = 0;
00097         readpos = 0;
00098         endpos = num;
00099         while (readpos < endpos) {
00100                 if (v[readpos] < pivotval) {
00101                         v[frontpos++] = v[readpos++];
00102                 }
00103                 else if (v[readpos] == pivotval) {
00104                         readpos++;
00105                         pivotcount++;
00106                 }
00107                 else {
00108                         tmp = v[--endpos];
00109                         v[endpos] = v[readpos];
00110                         v[readpos] = tmp;
00111                 }
00112         }
00113         assert(readpos == endpos);
00114         assert(frontpos + pivotcount == readpos);
00115 
00116         for (i=frontpos; i<endpos; i++) {
00117                 v[i] = pivotval;
00118         }
00119 
00120         for (i=endpos, j=num-1; i<j; i++,j--) {
00121                 tmp = v[i];
00122                 v[i] = v[j];
00123                 v[j] = tmp;
00124         }
00125 
00126         sortints(v, frontpos);
00127         sortints(&v[endpos], num-endpos);
00128 }
00129 
00131 
00132 static
00133 void
00134 initprogname(const char *av0)
00135 {
00136         if (av0) {
00137                 progname = strrchr(av0, '/');
00138                 if (progname) {
00139                         progname++;
00140                 }
00141                 else {
00142                         progname = av0;
00143                 }
00144         }
00145         else {
00146                 progname = "psort";
00147         }
00148 }
00149 
00150 static
00151 void
00152 vscomplain(char *buf, size_t len, const char *fmt, va_list ap, int err)
00153 {
00154         size_t pos;
00155 
00156         if (me >= 0) {
00157                 snprintf(buf, len, "%s: proc %d: ", progname, me);
00158         }
00159         else {
00160                 snprintf(buf, len, "%s: ", progname);
00161         }
00162         pos = strlen(buf);
00163 
00164         vsnprintf(buf+pos, len-pos, fmt, ap);
00165         pos = strlen(buf);
00166 
00167         if (err >= 0) {
00168                 snprintf(buf+pos, len-pos, ": %s\n", strerror(err));
00169         }
00170         else {
00171                 snprintf(buf+pos, len-pos, "\n");
00172         }
00173 }
00174 
00175 static
00176 void
00177 complainx(const char *fmt, ...)
00178 {
00179         char buf[256];
00180         va_list ap;
00181 
00182         va_start(ap, fmt);
00183         vscomplain(buf, sizeof(buf), fmt, ap, -1);
00184         va_end(ap);
00185 
00186         /* Write the message in one go so it's atomic */
00187         write(STDERR_FILENO, buf, strlen(buf));
00188 }
00189 
00190 static
00191 void
00192 complain(const char *fmt, ...)
00193 {
00194         char buf[256];
00195         va_list ap;
00196         int err = errno;
00197 
00198         va_start(ap, fmt);
00199         vscomplain(buf, sizeof(buf), fmt, ap, err);
00200         va_end(ap);
00201 
00202         /* Write the message in one go so it's atomic */
00203         write(STDERR_FILENO, buf, strlen(buf));
00204 }
00205 
00207 
00208 static
00209 int
00210 doopen(const char *path, int flags, int mode)
00211 {
00212         int fd;
00213 
00214         fd = open(path, flags, mode);
00215         if (fd<0) {
00216                 complain("%s", path);
00217                 exit(1);
00218         }
00219         return fd;
00220 }
00221 
00222 static
00223 void
00224 doclose(const char *path, int fd)
00225 {
00226         if (close(fd)) {
00227                 complain("%s: close", path);
00228                 exit(1);
00229         }
00230 }
00231 
00232 static
00233 void
00234 docreate(const char *path)
00235 {
00236         int fd;
00237 
00238         fd = doopen(path, O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC, 0664);
00239         doclose(path, fd);
00240 }
00241 
00242 static
00243 void
00244 doremove(const char *path)
00245 {
00246         static int noremove;
00247 
00248         if (noremove) {
00249                 return;
00250         }
00251 
00252         if (remove(path) < 0) {
00253                 if (errno == ENOSYS) {
00254                         /* Complain (and try) only once. */
00255                         noremove = 1;
00256                 }
00257                 complain("%s: remove", path);
00258         }
00259 }
00260 
00261 static
00262 off_t
00263 getsize(const char *path)
00264 {
00265         struct stat buf;
00266         int fd;
00267         static int no_stat, no_fstat;
00268 
00269         if (!no_stat) {
00270                 if (stat(path, &buf) == 0) {
00271                         return buf.st_size;
00272                 }
00273                 if (errno != ENOSYS) {
00274                         complain("%s: stat", path);
00275                         exit(1);
00276                 }
00277                 /* Avoid further "Unknown syscall 81" messages */
00278                 no_stat = 1;
00279         }
00280 
00281         fd = doopen(path, O_RDONLY, 0);
00282         if (!no_fstat) {
00283                 if (fstat(fd, &buf) == 0) {
00284                         close(fd);
00285                         return buf.st_size;
00286                 }
00287                 if (errno != ENOSYS) {
00288                         complain("%s: stat", path);
00289                         exit(1);
00290                 }
00291                 /* Avoid further "Unknown syscall 82" messages */
00292                 no_fstat = 1;
00293         }
00294 
00295         /* otherwise, lseek */
00296         if (lseek(fd, 0, SEEK_END) >= 0) {
00297                 buf.st_size = lseek(fd, 0, SEEK_CUR);
00298                 if (buf.st_size >= 0) {
00299                         return buf.st_size;
00300                 }
00301         }
00302         complain("%s: getting file size with lseek", path);
00303         close(fd);
00304         exit(1);
00305 }
00306 
00307 static
00308 size_t
00309 doread(const char *path, int fd, void *buf, size_t len)
00310 {
00311         int result;
00312 
00313         result = read(fd, buf, len);
00314         if (result < 0) {
00315                 complain("%s: read", path);
00316                 exit(1);
00317         }
00318         return (size_t) result;
00319 }
00320 
00321 static
00322 void
00323 doexactread(const char *path, int fd, void *buf, size_t len)
00324 {
00325         size_t result;
00326 
00327         result = doread(path, fd, buf, len);
00328         if (result != len) {
00329                 complainx("%s: read: short count", path);
00330                 exit(1);
00331         }
00332 }
00333 
00334 static
00335 void
00336 dowrite(const char *path, int fd, const void *buf, size_t len)
00337 {
00338         int result;
00339 
00340         result = write(fd, buf, len);
00341         if (result < 0) {
00342                 complain("%s: write", path);
00343                 exit(1);
00344         }
00345         if ((size_t) result != len) {
00346                 complainx("%s: write: short count", path);
00347                 exit(1);
00348         }
00349 }
00350 
00351 static
00352 void
00353 dolseek(const char *name, int fd, off_t offset, int whence)
00354 {
00355         if (lseek(fd, offset, whence) < 0) {
00356                 complain("%s: lseek", name);
00357                 exit(1);
00358         }
00359 }
00360 
00361 #if 0 /* let's not require subdirs */
00362 static
00363 void
00364 dochdir(const char *path)
00365 {
00366         if (chdir(path) < 0) {
00367                 complain("%s: chdir", path);
00368                 exit(1);
00369         }
00370 }
00371 
00372 static
00373 void
00374 domkdir(const char *path, int mode)
00375 {
00376         if (mkdir(path, mode) < 0) {
00377                 complain("%s: mkdir", path);
00378                 exit(1);
00379         }
00380 }
00381 #endif /* 0 */
00382 
00383 static
00384 pid_t
00385 dofork(void)
00386 {
00387         pid_t pid;
00388 
00389         pid = fork();
00390         if (pid < 0) {
00391                 complain("fork");
00392                 /* but don't exit */
00393         }
00394 
00395         return pid;
00396 }
00397 
00399 
00400 static
00401 int
00402 dowait(int guy, pid_t pid)
00403 {
00404         int status, result;
00405 
00406         result = waitpid(pid, &status, 0);
00407         if (result < 0) {
00408                 complain("waitpid");
00409                 return -1;
00410         }
00411         if (WIFSIGNALED(status)) {
00412                 complainx("proc %d: signal %d", guy, WTERMSIG(status));
00413                 return -1;
00414         }
00415         assert(WIFEXITED(status));
00416         status = WEXITSTATUS(status);
00417         if (status) {
00418                 complainx("proc %d: exit %d", guy, status);
00419                 return -1;
00420         }
00421         return 0;
00422 }
00423 
00424 static
00425 void
00426 doforkall(const char *phasename, void (*func)(void))
00427 {
00428         int i, bad = 0;
00429         pid_t pids[numprocs];
00430 
00431         for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00432                 pids[i] = dofork();
00433                 if (pids[i] < 0) {
00434                         bad = 1;
00435                 }
00436                 else if (pids[i] == 0) {
00437                         /* child */
00438                         me = i;
00439                         func();
00440                         exit(0);
00441                 }
00442         }
00443 
00444         for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00445                 if (pids[i] > 0 && dowait(i, pids[i])) {
00446                         bad = 1;
00447                 }
00448         }
00449 
00450         if (bad) {
00451                 complainx("%s failed.", phasename);
00452                 exit(1);
00453         }
00454 }
00455 
00456 static
00457 void
00458 seekmyplace(const char *name, int fd)
00459 {
00460         int keys_per, myfirst;
00461         off_t offset;
00462 
00463         keys_per = numkeys / numprocs;
00464         myfirst = me*keys_per;
00465         offset = myfirst * sizeof(int);
00466 
00467         dolseek(name, fd, offset, SEEK_SET);
00468 }
00469 
00470 static
00471 int
00472 getmykeys(void)
00473 {
00474         int keys_per, myfirst, mykeys;
00475 
00476         keys_per = numkeys / numprocs;
00477         myfirst = me*keys_per;
00478         mykeys = (me < numprocs-1) ? keys_per : numkeys - myfirst;
00479 
00480         return mykeys;
00481 }
00482 
00484 
00485 static
00486 unsigned long
00487 checksum_file(const char *path)
00488 {
00489         int fd;
00490         char buf[512];
00491         size_t count, i;
00492         unsigned long sum = 0;
00493 
00494         fd = doopen(path, O_RDONLY, 0);
00495 
00496         while ((count = doread(path, fd, buf, sizeof(buf))) > 0) {
00497                 for (i=0; i<count; i++) {
00498                         sum += (unsigned char) buf[i];
00499                 }
00500         }
00501 
00502         doclose(path, fd);
00503 
00504         return sum;
00505 }
00506 
00508 
00509 static long *seeds;
00510 
00511 static
00512 void
00513 genkeys_sub(void)
00514 {
00515         int fd, i, mykeys, keys_done, keys_to_do, value;
00516 
00517         fd = doopen(PATH_KEYS, O_WRONLY, 0);
00518 
00519         mykeys = getmykeys();
00520         seekmyplace(PATH_KEYS, fd);
00521 
00522         srandom(seeds[me]);
00523         keys_done = 0;
00524         while (keys_done < mykeys) {
00525                 keys_to_do = mykeys - keys_done;
00526                 if (keys_to_do > WORKNUM) {
00527                         keys_to_do = WORKNUM;
00528                 }
00529 
00530                 for (i=0; i<keys_to_do; i++) {
00531                         value = random();
00532 
00533                         // check bounds of value
00534                         assert(value >= 0);
00535                         assert(value <= RANDOM_MAX);
00536 
00537                         // do not allow the value to be zero or RANDOM_MAX
00538                         while (value == 0 || value == RANDOM_MAX) {
00539                                 value = random();
00540                         }
00541 
00542                         workspace[i] = value;
00543                 }
00544 
00545                 dowrite(PATH_KEYS, fd, workspace, keys_to_do*sizeof(int));
00546                 keys_done += keys_to_do;
00547         }
00548 
00549         doclose(PATH_KEYS, fd);
00550 }
00551 
00552 static
00553 void
00554 genkeys(void)
00555 {
00556         long seedspace[numprocs];
00557         int i;
00558 
00559         /* Create the file. */
00560         docreate(PATH_KEYS);
00561 
00562         /* Generate random seeds for each subprocess. */
00563         srandom(randomseed);
00564         for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00565                 seedspace[i] = random();
00566         }
00567 
00568         /* Do it. */
00569         seeds = seedspace;
00570         doforkall("Initialization", genkeys_sub);
00571         seeds = NULL;
00572 
00573         /* Cross-check the size of the output. */
00574         if (getsize(PATH_KEYS) != correctsize) {
00575                 complainx("%s: file is wrong size", PATH_KEYS);
00576                 exit(1);
00577         }
00578 
00579         /* Checksum the output. */
00580         checksum = checksum_file(PATH_KEYS);
00581         complainx("Checksum of unsorted keys: %ld", checksum);
00582 }
00583 
00585 
00586 static
00587 const char *
00588 binname(int a, int b)
00589 {
00590         static char rv[32];
00591         snprintf(rv, sizeof(rv), "bin-%d-%d", a, b);
00592         return rv;
00593 }
00594 
00595 static
00596 const char *
00597 mergedname(int a)
00598 {
00599         static char rv[32];
00600         snprintf(rv, sizeof(rv), "merged-%d", a);
00601         return rv;
00602 }
00603 
00604 static
00605 void
00606 bin(void)
00607 {
00608         int infd, outfds[numprocs];
00609         const char *name;
00610         int i, mykeys, keys_done, keys_to_do;
00611         int key, pivot, binnum;
00612 
00613         infd = doopen(PATH_KEYS, O_RDONLY, 0);
00614 
00615         mykeys = getmykeys();
00616         seekmyplace(PATH_KEYS, infd);
00617 
00618         for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00619                 name = binname(me, i);
00620                 outfds[i] = doopen(name, O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC, 0664);
00621         }
00622 
00623         pivot = (RANDOM_MAX / numprocs);
00624 
00625         keys_done = 0;
00626         while (keys_done < mykeys) {
00627                 keys_to_do = mykeys - keys_done;
00628                 if (keys_to_do > WORKNUM) {
00629                         keys_to_do = WORKNUM;
00630                 }
00631 
00632                 doexactread(PATH_KEYS, infd, workspace,
00633                             keys_to_do * sizeof(int));
00634 
00635                 for (i=0; i<keys_to_do; i++) {
00636                         key = workspace[i];
00637 
00638                         binnum = key / pivot;
00639                         if (key <= 0) {
00640                                 complainx("proc %d: garbage key %d", me, key);
00641                                 key = 0;
00642                         }
00643                         assert(binnum >= 0);
00644                         assert(binnum < numprocs);
00645                         dowrite("bin", outfds[binnum], &key, sizeof(key));
00646                 }
00647 
00648                 keys_done += keys_to_do;
00649         }
00650         doclose(PATH_KEYS, infd);
00651 
00652         for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00653                 doclose(binname(me, i), outfds[i]);
00654         }
00655 }
00656 
00657 static
00658 void
00659 sortbins(void)
00660 {
00661         const char *name;
00662         int i, fd;
00663         off_t binsize;
00664 
00665         for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00666                 name = binname(me, i);
00667                 binsize = getsize(name);
00668                 if (binsize % sizeof(int) != 0) {
00669                         complainx("%s: bin size %ld no good", name,
00670                                   (long) binsize);
00671                         exit(1);
00672                 }
00673                 if (binsize > (off_t) sizeof(workspace)) {
00674                         complainx("proc %d: %s: bin too large", me, name);
00675                         exit(1);
00676                 }
00677 
00678                 fd = doopen(name, O_RDWR, 0);
00679                 doexactread(name, fd, workspace, binsize);
00680 
00681                 sortints(workspace, binsize/sizeof(int));
00682 
00683                 dolseek(name, fd, 0, SEEK_SET);
00684                 dowrite(name, fd, workspace, binsize);
00685                 doclose(name, fd);
00686         }
00687 }
00688 
00689 static
00690 void
00691 mergebins(void)
00692 {
00693         int infds[numprocs], outfd;
00694         int values[numprocs], ready[numprocs];
00695         const char *name, *outname;
00696         int i, result;
00697         int numready, place, val, worknum;
00698 
00699         outname = mergedname(me);
00700         outfd = doopen(outname, O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC, 0664);
00701 
00702         for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00703                 name = binname(i, me);
00704                 infds[i] = doopen(name, O_RDONLY, 0);
00705                 values[i] = 0;
00706                 ready[i] = 0;
00707         }
00708 
00709         worknum = 0;
00710 
00711         while (1) {
00712                 numready = 0;
00713                 for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00714                         if (infds[i] < 0) {
00715                                 continue;
00716                         }
00717 
00718                         if (!ready[i]) {
00719                                 result = doread("bin", infds[i], 
00720                                                 &val, sizeof(int));
00721                                 if (result == 0) {
00722                                         doclose("bin", infds[i]);
00723                                         infds[i] = -1;
00724                                         continue;
00725                                 }
00726                                 if ((size_t) result != sizeof(int)) {
00727                                         complainx("%s: read: short count",
00728                                                   binname(i, me));
00729                                         exit(1);
00730                                 }
00731                                 values[i] = val;
00732                                 ready[i] = 1;
00733                         }
00734                         numready++;
00735                 }
00736                 if (numready == 0) {
00737                         break;
00738                 }
00739 
00740                 /* find the smallest */
00741                 place = -1;
00742                 for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00743                         if (!ready[i]) {
00744                                 continue;
00745                         }
00746                         if (place < 0 || values[i] < val) {
00747                                 val = values[i];
00748                                 place = i;
00749                         }
00750                 }
00751                 assert(place >= 0);
00752 
00753                 workspace[worknum++] = val;
00754                 if (worknum >= WORKNUM) {
00755                         assert(worknum == WORKNUM);
00756                         dowrite(outname, outfd, workspace,
00757                                 worknum * sizeof(int));
00758                         worknum = 0;
00759                 }
00760                 ready[place] = 0;
00761         }
00762 
00763         dowrite(outname, outfd, workspace, worknum * sizeof(int));
00764         doclose(outname, outfd);
00765 
00766         for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00767                 assert(infds[i] < 0);
00768         }
00769 }
00770 
00771 static
00772 void
00773 assemble(void)
00774 {
00775         off_t mypos;
00776         int i, fd;
00777         const char *args[3];
00778 
00779         mypos = 0;
00780         for (i=0; i<me; i++) {
00781                 mypos += getsize(mergedname(i));
00782         }
00783 
00784         fd = doopen(PATH_SORTED, O_WRONLY, 0);
00785         dolseek(PATH_SORTED, fd, mypos, SEEK_SET);
00786 
00787         if (dup2(fd, STDOUT_FILENO) < 0) {
00788                 complain("dup2");
00789                 exit(1);
00790         }
00791 
00792         doclose(PATH_SORTED, fd);
00793 
00794         args[0] = "cat";
00795         args[1] = mergedname(me);
00796         args[2] = NULL;
00797         execv("/bin/cat", (char **) args);
00798         complain("/bin/cat: exec");
00799         exit(1);
00800 }
00801 
00802 static
00803 void
00804 checksize_bins(void)
00805 {
00806         off_t totsize;
00807         int i, j;
00808 
00809         totsize = 0;
00810         for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00811                 for (j=0; j<numprocs; j++) {
00812                         totsize += getsize(binname(i, j));
00813                 }
00814         }
00815         if (totsize != correctsize) {
00816                 complain("Sum of bin sizes is wrong (%ld, should be %ld)",
00817                          (long) totsize, (long) correctsize);
00818                 exit(1);
00819         }
00820 }
00821 
00822 static
00823 void
00824 checksize_merge(void)
00825 {
00826         off_t totsize;
00827         int i;
00828 
00829         totsize = 0;
00830         for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00831                 totsize += getsize(mergedname(i));
00832         }
00833         if (totsize != correctsize) {
00834                 complain("Sum of merged sizes is wrong (%ld, should be %ld)",
00835                          (long) totsize, (long) correctsize);
00836                 exit(1);
00837         }
00838 }
00839 
00840 static
00841 void
00842 sort(void)
00843 {
00844         unsigned long sortedsum;
00845         int i, j;
00846 
00847         /* Step 1. Toss into bins. */
00848         doforkall("Tossing", bin);
00849         checksize_bins();
00850         complainx("Done tossing into bins.");
00851 
00852         /* Step 2: Sort the bins. */
00853         doforkall("Sorting", sortbins);
00854         checksize_bins();
00855         complainx("Done sorting the bins.");
00856 
00857         /* Step 3: Merge corresponding bins. */
00858         doforkall("Merging", mergebins);
00859         checksize_merge();
00860         complainx("Done merging the bins.");
00861 
00862         /* Step 3a: delete the bins */
00863         for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00864                 for (j=0; j<numprocs; j++) {
00865                         doremove(binname(i, j));
00866                 }
00867         }
00868 
00869         /* Step 4: assemble output file */
00870         docreate(PATH_SORTED);
00871         doforkall("Final assembly", assemble);
00872         if (getsize(PATH_SORTED) != correctsize) {
00873                 complainx("%s: file is wrong size", PATH_SORTED);
00874                 exit(1);
00875         }
00876 
00877         /* Step 4a: delete the merged bins */
00878         for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00879                 doremove(mergedname(i));
00880         }
00881 
00882         /* Step 5: Checksum the result. */
00883         sortedsum = checksum_file(PATH_SORTED);
00884         complainx("Checksum of sorted keys: %ld", sortedsum);
00885 
00886         if (sortedsum != checksum) {
00887                 complainx("Sums do not match");
00888                 exit(1);
00889         }
00890 }
00891 
00893 
00894 static
00895 const char *
00896 validname(int a)
00897 {
00898         static char rv[32];
00899         snprintf(rv, sizeof(rv), "valid-%d", a);
00900         return rv;
00901 }
00902 
00903 static
00904 void
00905 checksize_valid(void)
00906 {
00907         off_t totvsize, correctvsize;
00908         int i;
00909 
00910         correctvsize = (off_t) numprocs*2*sizeof(int);
00911 
00912         totvsize = 0;
00913         for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00914                 totvsize += getsize(validname(i));
00915         }
00916         if (totvsize != correctvsize) {
00917                 complainx("Sum of validation sizes is wrong "
00918                           "(%ld, should be %ld)",
00919                           (long) totvsize, (long) correctvsize);
00920                 exit(1);
00921         }
00922 }
00923 
00924 static
00925 void
00926 dovalidate(void)
00927 {
00928         const char *name;
00929         int fd, i, mykeys, keys_done, keys_to_do;
00930         int key, smallest, largest;
00931 
00932         name = PATH_SORTED;
00933         fd = doopen(name, O_RDONLY, 0);
00934 
00935         mykeys = getmykeys();
00936         seekmyplace(name, fd);
00937 
00938         smallest = RANDOM_MAX;
00939         largest = 0;
00940 
00941         keys_done = 0;
00942         while (keys_done < mykeys) {
00943                 keys_to_do = mykeys - keys_done;
00944                 if (keys_to_do > WORKNUM) {
00945                         keys_to_do = WORKNUM;
00946                 }
00947 
00948                 doexactread(name, fd, workspace, keys_to_do * sizeof(int));
00949 
00950                 for (i=0; i<keys_to_do; i++) {
00951                         key = workspace[i];
00952 
00953                         if (key < 0) {
00954                                 complain("%s: found negative key", name);
00955                                 exit(1);
00956                         }
00957                         if (key == 0) {
00958                                 complain("%s: found zero key", name);
00959                                 exit(1);
00960                         }
00961                         if (key >= RANDOM_MAX) {
00962                                 complain("%s: found too-large key", name);
00963                                 exit(1);
00964                         }
00965 
00966                         if (key < smallest) {
00967                                 smallest = key;
00968                         }
00969                         if (key > largest) {
00970                                 largest = key;
00971                         }
00972                 }
00973 
00974                 keys_done += keys_to_do;
00975         }
00976         doclose(name, fd);
00977 
00978         name = validname(me);
00979         fd = doopen(name, O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC, 0664);
00980         dowrite(name, fd, &smallest, sizeof(smallest));
00981         dowrite(name, fd, &largest, sizeof(largest));
00982         doclose(name, fd);
00983 }
00984 
00985 static
00986 void
00987 validate(void)
00988 {
00989         int smallest, largest, prev_largest;
00990         int i, fd;
00991         const char *name;
00992 
00993         doforkall("Validation", dovalidate);
00994         checksize_valid();
00995 
00996         prev_largest = 1;
00997 
00998         for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
00999                 name = validname(i);
01000                 fd = doopen(name, O_RDONLY, 0);
01001 
01002                 doexactread(name, fd, &smallest, sizeof(int));
01003                 doexactread(name, fd, &largest, sizeof(int));
01004 
01005                 if (smallest < 1) {
01006                         complainx("Validation: block %d: bad SMALLEST", i);
01007                         exit(1);
01008                 }
01009                 if (largest >= RANDOM_MAX) {
01010                         complainx("Validation: block %d: bad LARGEST", i);
01011                         exit(1);
01012                 }
01013                 if (smallest > largest) {
01014                         complainx("Validation: block %d: SMALLEST > LARGEST",
01015                                   i);
01016                         exit(1);
01017                 }
01018 
01019                 if (smallest < prev_largest) {
01020                         complain("Validation: block %d smallest key %d",
01021                                  i, smallest);
01022                         complain("Validation: previous block largest key %d",
01023                                  prev_largest);
01024                         complain("Validation failed");
01025                         exit(1);
01026                 }
01027         }
01028 
01029 
01030         for (i=0; i<numprocs; i++) {
01031                 doremove(validname(i));
01032         }
01033 }
01034 
01036 
01037 static
01038 void
01039 setdir(void)
01040 {
01041 #if 0 /* let's not require subdirs */
01042         domkdir(PATH_TESTDIR, 0775);
01043         dochdir(PATH_TESTDIR);
01044 #endif /* 0 */
01045 }
01046 
01047 static
01048 void
01049 unsetdir(void)
01050 {
01051         doremove(PATH_KEYS);
01052         doremove(PATH_SORTED);
01053 #if 0 /* let's not require subdirs */
01054         dochdir("..");
01055 
01056         if (rmdir(PATH_TESTDIR) < 0) {
01057                 complain("%s: rmdir", PATH_TESTDIR);
01058                 /* but don't exit */
01059         }
01060 #endif /* 0 */
01061 }
01062 
01064 
01065 static
01066 void
01067 randomize(void)
01068 {
01069         int fd;
01070 
01071         fd = doopen(PATH_RANDOM, O_RDONLY, 0);
01072         doexactread(PATH_RANDOM, fd, &randomseed, sizeof(randomseed));
01073         doclose(PATH_RANDOM, fd);
01074 }
01075 
01076 static
01077 void
01078 usage(void)
01079 {
01080         complain("Usage: %s [-p procs] [-k keys] [-s seed] [-r]", progname);
01081         exit(1);
01082 }
01083 
01084 static
01085 void
01086 doargs(int argc, char *argv[])
01087 {
01088         int i, ch, val, arg;
01089 
01090         for (i=1; i<argc; i++) {
01091                 if (argv[i][0] != '-') {
01092                         usage();
01093                         return;
01094                 }
01095                 ch = argv[i][1];
01096                 switch (ch) {
01097                     case 'p': arg = 1; break;
01098                     case 'k': arg = 1; break;
01099                     case 's': arg = 1; break;
01100                     case 'r': arg = 0; break;
01101                     default: usage(); return;
01102                 }
01103                 if (arg) {
01104                         if (argv[i][2]) {
01105                                 val = atoi(argv[i]+2);
01106                         }
01107                         else {
01108                                 i++;
01109                                 if (!argv[i]) {
01110                                         complain("Option -%c requires an "
01111                                                  "argument", ch);
01112                                         exit(1);
01113                                 }
01114                                 val = atoi(argv[i]);
01115                         }
01116                         switch (ch) {
01117                             case 'p': numprocs = val; break;
01118                             case 'k': numkeys = val; break;
01119                             case 's': randomseed = val; break;
01120                             default: assert(0); break;
01121                         }
01122                 }
01123                 else {
01124                         switch (ch) {
01125                             case 'r': randomize(); break;
01126                             default: assert(0); break;
01127                         }
01128                 }
01129         }
01130 }
01131 
01132 int
01133 main(int argc, char *argv[])
01134 {
01135         initprogname(argc > 0 ? argv[0] : NULL);
01136 
01137         doargs(argc, argv);
01138         correctsize = (off_t) (numkeys*sizeof(int));
01139 
01140         setdir();
01141 
01142         genkeys();
01143         sort();
01144         validate();
01145         complainx("Succeeded.");
01146 
01147         unsetdir();
01148 
01149         return 0;
01150 }
Generated on Sun Jun 27 22:12:14 2010 for Os/161 by  1.6.1
